openmeter/productcatalog/plan/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing plan.Repository for plan lifecycle persistence (CRUD, phase management, rate card bulk creation). Primary constraint: all mutations must wrap Ent calls in entutils.TransactingRepo to honor ctx-carried transactions.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping
patterns.description: Every adapter method body must be a closure passed to entutils.TransactingRepo[T, *adapter](ctx, a, fn) so the call rebinds to any transaction already in ctx.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() on construction
patterns.description: Constructor New(Config) calls config.Validate() first and returns error if Client or Logger are nil; Config implements models.Validator.

patterns.name: WithTx / Self pattern
patterns.description: adapter implements WithTx(ctx, *TxDriver) *adapter and Self() *adapter so entutils.TransactingRepo can rebind the db field to a tx-scoped client.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Eager load with typed helper vars
patterns.description: Reusable query modifier vars (planPhaseEagerLoadRateCardsFn, rateCardEagerLoadFeaturesFn, rateCardEagerLoadTaxCodesFn, planEagerLoadActiveAddons) compose WithRatecards/WithFeatures/WithTaxCode so every query loads the full graph consistently.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt
patterns.description: DeletePlan sets DeletedAt timestamp; phases are intentionally NOT soft-deleted so state before deletion remains inspectable. Queries filter with plandb.DeletedAtIsNil() unless IncludeDeleted is true.

patterns.name: Status filtering via clock.Now()
patterns.description: ListPlans maps PlanStatus enum values to EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo predicates using clock.Now().UTC() so tests can override time via clock.SetTime.
patterns.example: now := clock.Now().UTC(); predicates = append(predicates, plandb.And(plandb.EffectiveFromLTE(now), plandb.Or(plandb.EffectiveToGTE(now), plandb.EffectiveToIsNil())))

patterns.name: Bulk rate card create then re-query
patterns.description: After CreateBulk for rate cards, the phase row is re-fetched with eager loads (WithRatecards+WithFeatures+WithTaxCode) to return a fully populated struct instead of relying on in-memory assembly.

key_file_guides.role: Config, constructor, Tx/WithTx/Self methods — the transaction rebinding plumbing lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never store a tx-scoped client on the struct permanently; WithTx returns a new *adapter.

key_file_guides.role: ListPlans, CreatePlan, GetPlan, UpdatePlan, DeletePlan — all core CRUD wrapped in TransactingRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdatePlan phase replacement uses delete-all + re-create, not an incremental diff; callers must supply the full phase set.

key_file_guides.role: createPhase helper and rateCardBulkCreate — internal helpers used by CreatePlan and UpdatePlan.
key_file_guides.watch_for: rateCardBulkCreate accepts raw *entdb.PlanRateCardClient; ensure it is always called from within a TransactingRepo closure so it inherits the tx.

key_file_guides.role: FromPlanRow, FromPlanPhaseRow, fromPlanRateCardRow, asPlanRateCardRow — bidirectional Ent↔domain conversion. Checks Edges.AddonsOrErr() to distinguish 'not loaded' (nil) from 'loaded empty slice'.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New plan fields added to the Ent schema must be mapped here in both directions; missing field silently drops data.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly outside a TransactingRepo closure — bypasses ctx-carried transaction.
Assembling plan.Plan from in-memory state after CreateBulk instead of re-querying with eager loads.
Using entdb.IsNotFound without wrapping in plan.NewNotFoundError — callers expect domain errors.
Soft-deleting phases on plan deletion — breaks ability to inspect pre-deletion plan state (see comment in DeletePlan).
Directly editing openmeter/ent/db/ generated files — always regenerate with make generate.

decisions.decision: All methods wrap Ent access in entutils.TransactingRepo even for reads.
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate via ctx; not rebinding causes a helper to fall off the caller's transaction, risking partial writes.

decisions.decision: Phases are deleted and re-created wholesale on UpdatePlan when params.Phases != nil.
decisions.rationale: Simplifies correctness over incremental diffing; callers that only change plan metadata pass nil Phases to skip phase reconciliation.

decisions.decision: Status filtering built from clock predicates at query time rather than a stored status column.
decisions.rationale: Plan status is a derived function of EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo; no separate status field avoids dual-write consistency bugs.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new adapter method that reads and writes inside the caller's transaction

openmeter/productcatalog/plan/httpdriver.purpose: v1 HTTP handler layer for plan lifecycle endpoints, translating between api.* types and plan.Service calls via httptransport.Handler. Primary constraint: never call Ent or adapters directly — all persistence goes through plan.Service.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs
patterns.description: Each endpoint method returns a typed handler alias (e.g. ListPlansHandler = httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Req,Resp,Params]). The handler is built with a decoder closure, an operation closure delegating to h.service, a response encoder, and AppendOptions for operationName + ValidationErrorEncoder.

patterns.name: Namespace decoded from context
patterns.description: h.resolveNamespace(ctx) is called in every decoder; failure returns commonhttp.NewHTTPError(500) since the namespace must always be set by middleware.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Req{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace: %w", err) }

patterns.name: credits.Enabled guard in decoder
patterns.description: CreditOnlySettlementMode is rejected in CreatePlan and UpdatePlan decoders when h.credits.Enabled is false, producing models.NewGenericValidationError.
patterns.example: if !h.credits.Enabled && req.SettlementMode == productcatalog.CreditOnlySettlementMode { return CreatePlanRequest{}, models.NewGenericValidationError(...) }

patterns.name: Type alias pattern for request/response/handler
patterns.description: Each endpoint declares three type aliases (XxxRequest, XxxResponse, XxxHandler) at package level so the interface method signature is self-documenting.
patterns.example: type (ListPlansRequest = plan.ListPlansInput; ListPlansResponse = api.PlanPaginatedResponse; ListPlansHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...])

patterns.name: ref.ParseIDOrKey for dual ID/Key routing
patterns.description: GetPlan and NextPlan use ref.ParseIDOrKey to distinguish ULID IDs from string keys without separate endpoints.
patterns.example: idOrKey := ref.ParseIDOrKey(params.IDOrKey); return GetPlanRequest{NamespacedID: models.NamespacedID{Namespace: ns, ID: idOrKey.ID}, Key: idOrKey.Key, ...}

key_file_guides.role: Handler/PlanHandler interface declaration, handler struct, New constructor. Credits config injected here for settlement mode guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: credits appconfig.CreditsConfiguration must be passed to New; omitting it silently allows CreditOnly plans when credits are off.

key_file_guides.role: All endpoint implementations (ListPlans, CreatePlan, UpdatePlan, DeletePlan, GetPlan, PublishPlan, ArchivePlan, NextPlan).
key_file_guides.watch_for: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true is set in Create and Update decoders — do not remove, plans may have non-critical validation issues.

key_file_guides.role: FromPlan, FromPlanPhase, AsCreatePlanRequest, AsUpdatePlanRequest, AsPlanPhase — api↔domain conversion. Calls http.FromRateCard/AsRateCards for rate card mapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New plan fields must be mapped in both FromPlan and AsCreatePlanRequest/AsUpdatePlanRequest or the API silently ignores them.

anti_patterns: Importing entdb or calling plan.Repository directly from httpdriver — all access must go through plan.Service.
Hardcoding namespace strings instead of calling h.resolveNamespace(ctx).
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ValidationErrorEncoder(...)) — plan validation errors won't map to proper HTTP status codes.
Adding credits guard logic outside the decoder closure — guards belong in request decoding, not in the operation.

decisions.decision: Credits feature guard duplicated in both CreatePlan and UpdatePlan decoders.
decisions.rationale: credits.enabled must be enforced at the HTTP boundary; service layer does not know about deployment config.

decisions.decision: EffectivePeriod is zeroed in UpdatePlan service call; Publish/Archive are the only paths that change it.
decisions.rationale: Prevents callers from setting plan status directly via update; status transitions are gated through explicit PublishPlan/ArchivePlan operations.

openmeter/productcatalog/plan/service.purpose: Business-logic service implementing plan.Service: validates inputs, resolves feature/taxcode references, enforces status-based mutation guards, delegates to plan.Repository, and publishes domain events. Primary constraint: state transitions (publish/archive/next) are separate operations; UpdatePlan only modifies metadata and phases on Draft/Scheduled plans.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wrapping for mutations
patterns.description: All mutating methods wrap their closure in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, fn) to ensure atomicity across adapter calls and event publishing.

patterns.name: resolveFeatures before adapter calls
patterns.description: Before CreatePlan/UpdatePlan hits the adapter, s.resolveFeatures populates FeatureKey↔FeatureID cross-references by querying feature.FeatureConnector; missing features are converted to GenericValidationError.
patterns.example: if err = s.resolveFeatures(ctx, params.Namespace, &phase.RateCards); err != nil { if models.IsGenericNotFoundError(err) { err = models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }; return nil, ... }

patterns.name: Publish domain events after adapter write
patterns.description: Create/Update/Delete/Publish/Archive/Next all call s.publisher.Publish(ctx, plan.NewPlanXxxEvent(ctx, p)) after the adapter mutation succeeds, inside the transaction closure.
patterns.example: event := plan.NewPlanCreateEvent(ctx, p); if err := s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to publish plan created event: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Status-guard before mutations
patterns.description: UpdatePlan, DeletePlan, PublishPlan, ArchivePlan each check allowed statuses via lo.Contains before delegating to adapter; violations return models.NewGenericValidationError.
patterns.example: allowedPlanStatuses := []productcatalog.PlanStatus{productcatalog.PlanStatusDraft, productcatalog.PlanStatusScheduled}; if !lo.Contains(allowedPlanStatuses, planStatus) { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(...) }

patterns.name: Config-based constructor with nil checks
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all dependencies (Adapter, Feature, TaxCode, Logger, Publisher) are non-nil before returning the service.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, New constructor, service struct definition. All dependency fields (adapter, feature, taxCode, logger, publisher) are private.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new dependency requires updating Config, New validation, and service struct — all three must stay in sync.

key_file_guides.role: All plan.Service method implementations. resolveFeatures and resolveTaxCodes are private helpers called before adapter writes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreatePlan auto-increments version based on existing versions; callers should not pass a Version in CreatePlanInput.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests using pctestutils.NewTestEnv which wires the full stack (adapter + service). Tests drive behavior through env.Plan (service) not env.PlanRepository (adapter).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use context.Background() — in new tests prefer t.Context() per project convention.

anti_patterns: Calling s.adapter directly for mutations without wrapping in transaction.Run — breaks atomicity with event publishing.
Allowing EffectivePeriod to be set via UpdatePlan — it is explicitly zeroed to prevent direct status manipulation.
Skipping resolveFeatures before CreatePlan/UpdatePlan — rate cards with FeatureKey-only or FeatureID-only refs will have incomplete cross-references in the DB.
Publishing events outside the transaction closure — if the DB write succeeds but the event publish fails, the transaction rolls back only if both are inside transaction.Run.

decisions.decision: Feature and TaxCode resolution is done in service layer, not in the adapter.
decisions.rationale: Adapter only handles persistence; cross-entity lookups (feature by key/ID, tax code by stripe code) are orchestration concerns belonging in the service.

decisions.decision: Version is auto-incremented from existing versions; callers cannot set it directly.
decisions.rationale: Prevents version gaps and ensures monotonically increasing versions per key across create calls.

decisions.decision: Default settlement mode CreditThenInvoice is applied in service, not in the HTTP handler.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the default a domain invariant independent of the transport layer.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new mutating service method that calls the adapter and publishes an event
